title: Acetosella pentantha
taxonomy: Acetosella pentantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It belongs to the genus Acetosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as wood sorrels. The plant is native to parts of Africa, Asia, and Europe. Acetosella pentantha is a perennial herb with leaves that are alternate and have a distinctive shape. This species is often found in wetlands and woodlands, where it thrives in shaded areas.
uses: Acetosella pentantha is also known as the 'Sundew's cousin' due to its similar appearance, however, more research is required to confirm its exact medicinal uses. benefits: Acetosella pentantha, also known as the mountain sorrel, is believed to have various health benefits due to its high concentration of vitamins and minerals.
